description_long: This is a double-bladed axe with a broad, symmetrical steel head that features two curved cutting edges, each extending outward in a crescent shape. The blade has a weathered, darkened finish, suggesting heavy use or intentional aging for aesthetic effect. The handle is made of wood, displaying a natural, slightly twisted grain and a light brown color, with a visible texture that implies a comfortable grip. The handle is curved, which would aid in swinging and control, and appears to be securely fitted into the central eye of the axe head. No visible fasteners or modern attachments are present, indicating a traditional mounting style. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative engravings or branding visible.
description: A double-bladed, weathered steel axe with a curved wooden handle and a rugged, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, curved cutting edges of the double-bladed axe and the full width of the head.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the axe head and the curvature of the wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the opposite side of the double-bladed head and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim blade profile and the handle's curvature.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, cylindrical cross-section, and a slightly twisted ergonomic shape for grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bladed, symmetrical crescent-shaped steel head with two broad cutting edges and no beard or poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle fitted through the central eye of the blade.
